headlines.title: Oakland police respond to ‘critical incident'
headlines.description: Oakland police on Monday said they were responding to a “critical incident” in the area of 40th Avenue and International Boulevard. Police said Chief James Beere will address the incident at 4 p.m. Additional information wasn’t immediately available. This is a developing story. Details may change as more information becomes available. Stay tuned for updates. This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: 3 sailors rescued from San Francisco Bay after capsizing east of SFO
headlines.description: Three people were rescued from San Francisco Bay off San Mateo County on Saturday after their sailboat capsized. The trio was sailing near Coyote Point, which is south of San Francisco International Airport, when their boat tipped over at about 1:30 p.m. Deputies with the San Mateo County Sheriff’s Office Marine Unit who were training near the San Mateo-Hayward Bridge responded, the sheriff’s office said Monday. The boat capsized about a half mile east of the airport, the sheriff’s office said. The three sailors, who were Foster City residents, were cold from the water exposure but were otherwise uninjured. They were taken to Coyote Point Marina and evaluated by paramedics as a precaution. The boat was also towed to the marina by the sheriff’s office marine unit. Waters in that area of the Bay at that time were about 69 degrees, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.

headlines.title: Driver crashes into business in Novato
headlines.description: A driver crashed through a wall at a business in Novato Monday morning, according to police. Photos from the scene showed a sedan almost entirely inside HopMonk Tavern Novato, located at 224 Vintage Way. No injuries were reported. A cause for the crash wasn’t immediately known. HopMonk said the car crashed into the west wall of its music venue, forcing the business to temporarily close the space until the wall is fully repaired. HopMonk’s tavern and beer garden were not affected by the crash.

headlines.title: Watch live: Ledson Fire sparks evacuation order in Sonoma County
headlines.description: Here’s a look at the latest fire activity reported in the Bay Area. Ledson Fire burns about 13 acres in Santa Rosa Cal Fire crews on Monday afternoon responded to the so-called Ledson Fire in Santa Rosa. The blaze burned at least 13 acres along Sonomay Highway (Highway 12) and North Pythian Road. The area is near the Ledson Winery and Vineyards. The Sonoma County Sheriff’s Office has issued an evacuation order and is conducting evacuations for Zone SON-3H3. For information about evacuations please visit: https://protect.genasys.com/search As of 1:45 p.m., the blaze was spreading at a moderate rate and there was no containment. Additional air and ground resources have been requested, Cal Fire said. No injuries have been reported. The cause is under investigation. No other information was immediately available. Bay City News contributed to this report. PG&E Jul 11 San Francisco Jul 9 bay area weather Jul 9 This story uses functionality that may not work in our app. Click here to open the story in your web browser.

headlines.title: Economists say ‘we must act now' on AI's economic impact and job displacement risks
headlines.description: Hundreds of economists say in an open letter that institutions “must act now” to address how artificial intelligence could transform the economy and could put many people out of work. The statement released Monday was signed by top economists, along with computer scientists and some executives at tech companies including Anthropic, Google and OpenAI. “AI may become radically more powerful over the next 10 years,” says the letter organized by Stanford University’s digital economy lab. “This could drive an unprecedented transformation of our economy, larger than the Industrial Revolution, but unfolding over a vastly shorter time frame. It could bring risks, including large-scale job displacement, as well as opportunities such as major gains in living standards.” The letter, which has only four sentences, says leaders must “build the incentives, guardrails, and institutions needed to steer AI in a direction that complements humans and benefits society.” The Stanford lab says the letter has so far been signed by more than 200 economists and AI researchers, including 16 winners of a Nobel Prize. Computer scientist and AI pioneer Yoshua Bengio was among the signatories and said in a separate statement that based on the trajectory of AI development, “it is highly plausible that AI will drastically transform our economies.” “We must be intentional and make collective, democratic choices, rather than letting market forces play out and risking leaving most citizens behind,” wrote Bengio, a professor at the University of Montreal.

headlines.title: Red Cross faces blood shortage, asks for donors
headlines.description: The American Red Cross has announced an emergency blood shortage Monday and is asking for donors to donate blood, platelets, or plasma as soon as possible. According to the organization, there has been a steep decline in blood donations since the end of May this year, creating a gap between supply and patient need. As a thank-you to donors, the organization is giving away a Fandango Movie Ticket of up to $15 in ticket price and fees via email for whoever donates from July 13-31. More details are available at RedCrossBlood.org/July.

headlines.title: Fairfield police continue to investigate deadly graduation shooting following arrest
headlines.description: Fairfield police recently made an arrest in last month’s deadly shooting following a graduation ceremony. A 17-year-old boy was arrested in Texas Tuesday for allegedly killing 18-year-old Jamario Baker shortly after his graduation from Sem Yeto High School in Fairfield last month, Fairfield police announced Friday. The suspect, who police say fled Fairfield for Texas within days of the shooting, was arrested on suspicion of murder and related offenses in the Dallas-Fort Worth area Tuesday morning with the help of the United States Marshals Service. He will remain in custody in Texas pending his extradition to Solano County. Around 7:15 p.m. on June 3, gunfire broke out in the parking lot of Sem Yeto High School, which is shared with Fairfield High School, following a graduation ceremony for seniors of Sem Yeto that afternoon. The gunman shot Baker and three others, ages 11, 20 and 25, who survived. “Through the course of the investigation, detectives identified the juvenile suspect and determined that he had fled California and traveled to the State of Texas within days of the shooting,” Fairfield police wrote in a statement. “Investigators conducted dozens of witness interviews, reviewed hours of surveillance and digital video, analyzed photographs and electronic records, executed search warrants, processed forensic evidence, and coordinated efforts across multiple jurisdictions to develop a comprehensive and prosecutable case,” according to Fairfield police. Police were scheduled to hold a 4 p.m. Monday news conference about the investigation, but canceled after reporting there was no further updates to share. The investigation remains active despite the arrest, said police. Anyone with information is urged to contact the Fairfield Police Department Investigations Division at 707-428-7600 or by email at juneincidenttips@fairfield.ca.gov.

headlines.title: Oakland City Council to review Coliseum sale update
headlines.description: Oakland councilmembers will meet Monday to review the new proposed terms for the sale of the Coliseum complex. Leaders are considering a second amendment to the original deal that adds better financial terms for the city, reduces potential risks and preserves the original sale price of $125 million. The deal will also allow for an initial all-cash $50 million sale of the neighboring arena parcel, which, if approved, could happen early next year. The city has a 50% interest in the complex along with Alameda County, which has already approved its terms. Oakland Jul 9 Alameda County May 28 Alameda County supervisors approve new term sheet for sale of Coliseum property

headlines.title: Benjamin Netanyahu reacts to incident involving Rep. Ro Khanna in West Bank
headlines.description: On Saturday, U.S. Rep. Ro Khanna said he was detained by Israeli settlers on a recent trip to the occupied West Bank. On Sunday, he and Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu appeared on NBC’s Meet the Press, speaking about the incident. Democratic Congressman Ro Khanna, who represents part of the South Bay, was on his three-day visit to the West Bank this week, traveling there as part of a trip focused on hearing from Palestinians in the area. While visiting a Palestinian village, he said him and his delegation were surrounded by armed Israeli settlers. “I am certainly probably the first American politician who’s been detained by the IDF and Israeli settlers,” Khanna said. On Sunday’s NBC’S Meet the Press, Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu responded to the situation. “We’re a country of laws, and people who break the law, we take them to court,” he said. He said that there have been attempted attacks against Israelis in the West Bank, and there is vigilante effort, not by the settler community, but by what he called “delinquents”. “I don’t want vigilantes of any kind and we are working to put them under the law, but I think if we put things in perspective, we should see that we have thousands of attacks, and some few obvious breaking of the law, then we apply the full measure of the law against them,” he said. In a statement to NBC News, the Israeli Defense Forces say IDF troops were dispatched to the scene quickly, dispersed the Israeli civilians and reopened the blocked road. And that the IDF soldiers operating in the area did not take part in blocking the road. Khanna also appeared on Meet The Press, disputing that statement. “The IDF is lying what happened was unprecedented they had violent settlers detain American citizens, including an American government official, you had these settlers brandishing M4’s kicking the tires of our van, laughing at us mocking at us videotaping us we were detained for about 20 minutes fearful of our lives,” he said. Israeli police say officers witnessed no violence and that the group had entered a closed military zone before being released. Khanna says the group had to call the American embassy and were let through 75 minutes later. The incident comes at a time of shifting politics in the Democratic party as reports indicate Khanna says he’s considering a potential 2028 presidential run. Some say the visit was no surprise. “Even though it ended up in kind of a contentious result I think the take away for Khanna as he looks ahead and kind of bolsters his image to 2028 is that of a leading critic of Israel,” KQED Political Correspondent Guy Marzorati said. Khanna is also now calling on the prime minister to take action and investigate the incident.

headlines.title: Police investigate shooting that left man hurt in San Jose
headlines.description: San Jose police said they are investigating a shooting that sent a man to the hospital on Sunday. Police said they received a call just before noon regarding a shooting in the 2400 block of Amaryl Drive. At the scene, officers found a man with at least one gunshot wound, authorities said. Officials reported the man was taken to the hospital for non-life-threatening injuries. This is an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: Bay Area congressman says armed Israeli settlers held him in West Bank
headlines.description: U.S. Rep. Ro Khanna, D-Calif., says he was detained for more than an hour by armed Israeli settlers during a trip to the occupied West Bank, an encounter he said deepened his concerns about the conflict as he considers a 2028 presidential run. Khanna traveled to the region seeking an unfiltered look at life under Israeli occupation. Instead, he said he and his delegation were surrounded by settlers carrying American-made M4 rifles while visiting a destroyed Palestinian village. “We were just looking at it and these hoodlums come up with machine guns … and they detain us,” Khanna said. “They block off the road and then they call the IDF, and the IDF was on their side.” An aide traveling with the congressman said the group was held while members contacted the U.S. Embassy for help. The Israeli military said its troops responded after receiving reports that Israeli civilians were unlawfully blocking the road. The military said it dispersed the settlers and was not involved in detaining Khanna’s delegation. Israeli police said officers witnessed no violence, adding that the group had entered a closed military zone before being released. Khanna described the incident as an example of the “arrogance of power” operating with “total impunity.” “I saw the arrogance in the eyes of those settlers, 21- and 22-year-olds with guns, laughing that they had detained us,” Khanna said. “Having no respect that they were detaining Americans, no respect that there was an American congressperson in that bus.” The congressman later thanked a U.S. Embassy official for helping secure the group’s release and said he expects Israel to prosecute any violent settlers involved. The confrontation comes as the war in Gaza and conditions in the West Bank have emerged as sharp foreign policy divides within the Democratic Party. Khanna, who is seriously weighing a 2028 White House bid, said the experience has become a defining moment in how he views the region and his party’s future. “We need a new moral direction in our party, a new moral vision that respects the dignity and human rights of people around the world,” Khanna said. Guy Marzorati, a politics correspondent for KQED, noted that progressives like Khanna have used the conflict as a central issue in Democratic primaries across the country. “Khanna is someone who feels very comfortable talking about this issue of Israel, wanting to bring it front and center,” Marzorati said. “So clearly, looking ahead to 2028, this is something he’d want to bring front and center in a potential presidential campaign.” Israel has consistently rejected allegations of genocide in Gaza and apartheid in the West Bank, maintaining its military actions are necessary for national security.

headlines.title: Police bust illegal underground nightclub in San Jose
headlines.description: San Jose police busted an illegal, underground nightclub this weekend. SJPD officers began an extensive investigation following numerous complaints connected to an industrial building on Rogers Avenue. The department said it found an underground nightclub operating on the weekends, without any permits. On top of that, police said the club was not verifying ages at the door. Several crimes have been linked to the nightclub, including assault with a deadly weapon. The club is now closed. It’s not clear if police made any arrests.
